jobs.basic_qualifications:
- High school or equivalent
- A minimum of 1 year in an Environmental Health & Safety (EHS) related field OR; A Bachelor’s degree in EHS-relevant background OR; 6 months experience performing in a safety-related role within Amazon
- Knowledge of OSHA regulations specifically 29 CFR 1910
- Experience using Microsoft Word and Excel in a professional capacity
- Experience maintaining confidentiality in matter involving security and/or personnel issues in the workplace
jobs.description:
Join Amazon’s mission to become Earth’s safest place to work! At Amazon, we’ve set the ambitious goal to become the benchmark of safety excellence across all industries in which we operate. As a Workplace Health & Safety Specialist (WHSS), you'll be at the forefront of transforming workplace safety culture at one of the world's most innovative companies. Your expertise and dedication will directly protect employees, ensuring they return home safely to their families every day. You'll facilitate new approaches to safety, combining technology with human-centered solutions to create an environment where safety and productivity thrive together. This isn't just about maintaining safety standards – it's about revolutionizing how we think about and implement workplace safety, making a lasting impact on our people and operations. As your building's designated safety champion, you'll have the unique opportunity to build relationships, influence positive change, and help write the next chapter in Amazon's commitment to becoming Earth's Safest Place to Work.

Core Impact Areas:
Safety Program Excellence & Implementation - Drive comprehensive safety programs through data-driven strategies. Lead daily safety audits, incident investigations, and risk assessments while implementing innovative solutions to prevent injuries. Deliver engaging safety training that resonates with associates and promotes a proactive safety culture. Champion continuous improvement initiatives that enhance workplace safety while maintaining operational efficiency.

Associate Advocacy & Cultural Leadership - Serve as a trusted safety advisor and advocate for associate wellbeing, building strong partnerships across all levels of the organization. Foster a culture where safety is everyone's priority through effective communication, coaching, and positive reinforcement. Develop strong relationships with frontline associates and leadership to drive behavioral changes and ensure safety best practices become natural habits. Transform safety data into compelling narratives that influence positive change and drive engagement in safety initiatives.

Additional Job Elements include:

Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
Work in an environment where the noise level varies
Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)
Maintain effective care delivery in emergencies and assist emergency response at the site


Must be available to work flexible shifts including days, nights and/or weekends.

For those that do not have an adult First Aid, CPR, AED certification from the American Heart Association or American Red Cross, must obtain within 3 weeks of start date.

jobs.description:
Our vision is to be earth's most customer-centric company and earth's best employer. If you want to be part of an exciting business that aims to build a marketplace where customers can find and discover anything they might want to buy online, with a passion for delivering a great customer and seller experience, then this role is for you!

We are looking for high caliber and tested Specialist to support 3P business operations, to deliver initiatives and projects with strategic significance. Our ideal candidate is a problem solver, has investigative and critical thinking mindset, attentive to details, result-oriented, and capable of succeeding in a fast-paced environment, within minimal supervision.

This role requires excellent judgement to plan in order to accomplish goals and identify root causes that adversely impact seller experience, with the aim of driving timely resolutions with key partner teams. Ensure compliance with procedures and SLAs while achieving quality and performance metrics. Productivity and quality assurance will be evaluated along with the overall contribution to the development of the team.

Key job responsibilities
* Investigating escalated tickets received from Selling Partner Support (SPS) and track ticket resolution within defined SLAs.
* Conducting deep dives into Seller Contacts to identify seller intent/root-causes.
* Identifying top reason codes/labels driving seller contacts and work with product, program, partner teams to address gaps/eliminate defects
* Presenting insights to key stakeholders to implement improvement initiatives such as Selling Partner training, internal SOPs, updates to Seller facing help content. With the primary objective of resolving escalations, eliminating seller contacts and driving Root Cause Elimination initiatives.
* Work with product/program/partner teams to resolve escalated contact resolution of a supervisory or sensitive nature.

jobs.description:
The R2L Supply Chain team is looking for a dynamic Supply Chain Manager to own strategic and cross-functional operational initiatives that support the growth of our SSD and Perishable delivery business. In this role, you will use analytical, technical, program management, strategic and operations management skills to design, plan, and optimize network capacity while balancing Under-the-Roof (UTR) and On-The-Road (OTR) tradeoffs. This includes supporting business critical workstreams in support of key financial planning cycles and maximizing network capacity for an evolving network of FCs that serve our customers in the US. This role is expected to accelerate progress by driving crisp and timely decisions, clearing blockers (e.g. path to green), and escalating appropriately.

Key job responsibilities
• Lead supply chain modeling using proprietary tools
• Support manage complex supply chain initiatives with considerable impact
• Create data-driven analyses to support business decisions and growth strategies
• Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify root causes and implement scalable solutions
• Present findings and recommendations to senior leadership, writing strategic narratives and communicating across diverse functions
• Develop/implement mid-term supply chain strategies that balance speed, cost, and service performance
• Partner with cross-functional teams including Operations, Finance, Product, Technology, and Science to optimize supply chain performance


A day in the life
The Medium-Term Planning team bridges long-term planning and operational execution for all verticals – SSD Swift, Grocery FCs and RSR+ for Amazon's Rapid Rural Logistics (R2L) North America. We translate long-term capacity strategies into actionable quarterly plans that keep Amazon's last mile fulfillment network running efficiently, even during high-stakes events like Prime Week and Peak. Our team also manages long-term capacity and topology planning for Amazon Fresh in EU, UK, and JP plus Topology Planning for Whole Foods Market NA and Grocery Partnerships Worldwide.

About the team
Rapid & Rural Logistics (R2L) Supply Chain is responsible for the fulfillment strategy across the Amazon Faster-than-same-day Delivery network including capacity planning, network optimization, launch strategy and execution, and scenario-planning for our FCs. We are passionate about revolutionizing the delivery experience through innovative fulfillment solutions that enable speed. Our collaborative team combines analytical expertise with creative problem-solving to build and optimize our network to ensure we can maximize available capacity for customer demand. We partner closely with Retail, Finance, Real Estate, Tech, and Operations teams to create sustainable solutions that delight customers
jobs.description_short: The R2L Supply Chain team is looking for a dynamic Supply Chain Manager to own strategic and cross-functional operational initiatives that support the growth of our SSD and Perishable delivery business.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of experience in technology leadership roles within large enterprise betting and gaming organizations or gaming operators
- 5+ years of hands-on experience architecting complex betting and gaming solutions using AWS, IT/ISV, and multi-vendor environment technologies

- Deep understanding of regulatory requirements and compliance frameworks across multiple jurisdictions (e.g., UK Gambling Commission, Malta Gaming Authority, state-level US regulations)
jobs.company_name: Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL Greek Branch
jobs.description:
Are you a passionate builder with deep expertise in betting and gaming solutions and want to work with innovative companies transforming the industry? Amazon Web Services (AWS) has an exciting opportunity for you. The Media, Entertainment, Games & Sports team is seeking an experienced and motivated technologist with a passion for creating strategies, best practices, and solving complex business challenges focusing on the Betting and Gaming industry.

As a Senior Specialist Solutions Architect (SA) for Betting and Gaming at AWS, you'll be responsible for educating customers, partners, and internal stakeholders on emerging technologies and solutions that are transforming the Betting and Gaming industry in areas such as real-time wagering platforms, player engagement, responsible gaming, regulatory compliance, and advanced analytics. You get to work with sports betting operators, online casinos, lottery providers, fantasy sports platforms, and gaming technology vendors to help them build scalable, secure, and compliant solutions with the AWS cloud. You'll provide hands-on architectural guidance for real-life customer projects involving various AWS services and Independent Software Vendor (ISV)/partner solutions and collaborate with cross-Amazon teams to develop outcome-based holistic solutions for strategic opportunities in the betting and gaming industry.

In this role, you'll be a content creator, a storyteller, and a presenter, building and publishing content and collateral for betting and gaming customers in one-on-one and group settings. You'll work with customers remotely and in person, sharing your expertise and insights. This role requires at least 30% travel, including international travel.

Key job responsibilities
As a Leader, you'll anticipate future trends and identify applications of emerging technologies to enhance betting platforms, gaming operations, player personalization, and responsible gaming initiatives, enabling innovative experiences and effective solutions for our customers.

- Drive the strategy for betting and gaming solutions for customers across sports betting, online casino, lottery, and fantasy sports sectors.
- Architect and deliver innovative, customer-obsessed solutions leveraging AWS services and partner offerings tailored for the betting and gaming industry, with focus on real-time processing, scalability, security, and regulatory compliance.
- Create high-quality enablement content, including guidance, blogs, demos, POCs, and presentations, to share your expertise in betting and gaming solutions.
- Deliver written and spoken communications on complex betting platform architecture, gaming operations, player analytics, and regulatory compliance subjects, establishing AWS as the public cloud of choice for organizations in the betting and gaming industry looking to accelerate innovation.
- Provide technical leadership and mentorship to other Solutions Architects working with betting and gaming customers.

A day in the life
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

About the team
The AWS Industries team is committed to helping industry customers enable their digital transformation journeys. Our primary goal is to meet customers in their cloud journeys and work backwards from their industry-specific needs to transform their businesses and industries. Our team focuses on business outcomes and industry use cases on behalf of our customers, whether that is how we build products and solutions, how we sell, how we deliver, or how we partner.

jobs.description:
Are you interested in working on a fast-paced team that pioneers ways to delight customers? Do you have a passion for diving deep into data and crunching numbers? Are you motivated to work in challenging situations ? Do you have the grit required to deliver results no matter what? This is what the Central Flow team is all about! It’s always DAY ONE on this team and as such we look for ways to keep setting the bar higher. If you answered yes to the above questions, please keep reading!



The Central Flow Lead is responsible for managing volume and workflow of multiple Fulfillment Centers (FCs) from a remote centralized location.



This positions requires the candidate hired to work a mandatory night shift schedule and, if necessary, odd or long hours.



Responsibilities

· Manage the workflow of order across multiple processes.

· Manage customer experience while minimizing fulfillment cost across multiple FCs.

· Be responsible for communication of workflow status with Operations Managers and Area Managers and escalation of any Customer Experience risk, SLA failure risk, or deviation from Standard Work.

· Act as an advisor to the FC operations and senior operations team based on their holistic view of work flow throughout the FC.

· Facilitate communication between multiple areas of the operation.

· Provide operational insights to the Amazon Fulfillment Technology (AFT) team and work with them to develop advanced and intuitive automated flow control for FC operations.

· Manage flow settings for multiple process paths in outbound operations for multiple Fulfilment Centers based on shift plan.

· Understand the boundary of responsibilities between Operations on the floor and Central Flow as dictated by Standard Work developed by Central Flow Management.

· Execute outbound flow operations and communicate the Customer Experience risks and performance opportunities to FC operations in a standardized manner.

· Execute and develop shift plans based on FC operational requirements and adjust as variables change.

jobs.description:
AWS Fault Injection Service is a fully managed service for running fault injection experiments on AWS. FIS makes it easier to improve an application’s performance, observability, and resiliency. Fault injection experiments are used in chaos engineering, which is the practice of stressing an application in testing or production environments by creating disruptive events, such as sudden increase in CPU or memory consumption, observing how the system responds, and implementing improvements.

What does it take to succeed in this role?

We are looking for an experienced engineer to join our team, where you will have the opportunity to collaborate on delivering impactful customer facing product features across the full development lifecycle. You will own and implement specific console components, collaborate with UX and product defining new interfaces, and help to define the future of our product.

You should be comfortable working in an environment dedicated to meeting the expectations and requirements of customers. You should be intellectually curious, always looking to learn. You should be somebody who aspires to make the team both productive and a fun place to work. Being excited to learn from others while bringing your own novel capabilities and perspectives.

On-Call Responsibility

This position involves on-call responsibilities, every builder on FIS is also an owner. Nobody likes to get paged in the middle of the night or on the weekend, so we work to ensure that our systems are well engineered, fault tolerant and meet a high quality bar before the go into production. Our customers expect nothing less. When we do get paged we work together as a team to resolve the root cause of the issue and nobody is left on their own to deal with an operational issue.

If this sounds like the right challenge for you, then please apply today!

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.


About the team
ABOUT AWS:

Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of program or project management experience
- 1+ years of supply chain experience
- Experience as procurement/purchasing analyst, or other operational role within a procurement organization
- Experience working cross-functionally with tech and non-tech teams
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
- 1+ years of Excel (including VBA, pivot tables, array functions, power pivots, etc.) and data visualization tools such as Tableau experience
jobs.description:
Are you inspired by invention? Is problem-solving through teamwork in your DNA? Do you like the idea of seeing how your work impacts the bigger picture? Answer yes to any of these and you'll fit right in here at Amazon's Robotics Deployment and Packaging Innovation (RDPI). We are a smart team of doers that work passionately to apply advances in robotics and automation to solve real-world challenges that will transform our partners' experiences in ways we can't even imagine yet.

RDPI's Strategy & Operations (S&O) team is seeking a detail-oriented and driven Sourcing Manager to support our vendor and procurement operations across North America. This role is responsible for executing sourcing and procurement workflows that support the deployment of Amazon's robotics and packaging technologies through 3P integrator engagement. You will work within defined processes and strategies, contributing to vendor management activities, cost analysis, and procurement execution that keep our deployment programs on track.

This position requires up to 25% travel (domestic).

Key job responsibilities
- Execute the full Request for Quote (RFQ) process, including supporting Statement of Work (SOW) development in collaboration with stakeholders and assisting with bid evaluation and award
- Maintain and update procurement records, trackers, and workflow tools to ensure accurate documentation of day-to-day sourcing activities
- Support cost analysis activities, including gathering and organizing vendor pricing data to inform negotiation and base management strategies
- Assist in maintaining Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Work Instructions, and FAQs for sourcing and vendor management processes; ensure documentation is current and accessible
- Support vendor performance tracking by maintaining vendor data and contributing to KPI and metrics reporting
- Assist in resolving routine vendor issues, including price disputes and contract order tracking, escalating complex issues as appropriate
- Partner with cross-functional teams to understand deployment requirements and ensure vendor activities align with program needs
- Identify and flag risks and deviations in vendor performance or procurement workflows; escalate roadblocks to appropriate stakeholders
- Contribute to continuous improvement efforts by refining existing processes and supporting operational excellence initiatives


A day in the life
As a Sourcing Manager on the Vendor Strategy & Management team, your day centers on keeping our sourcing workflows running smoothly. You'll maintain procurement records, support RFQ execution, and help track vendor performance against defined metrics. You'll collaborate with deployment teams to understand their needs, assist in preparing cost analyses, and ensure SOPs reflect current processes. Whether you're updating a vendor tracker, supporting a bid evaluation, or helping resolve a pricing discrepancy, your attention to detail and follow-through directly support Amazon's ability to deploy robotics technology at scale.

Amazon offers a full range of benefits for you and eligible family members, including domestic partners and their children. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment. The benefits that generally apply to regular, full-time employees include:

1. Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
2. Maternity and Parental Leave Options
3. Paid Time Off (PTO)
4. 401(k) Plan

If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we'd still love to hear from you! At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skillsets.

About the team
S&O's Vendor Strategy & Management service formalizes external and internal partner engagement to drive safe, lean, efficient third-party vendor operations for technology deployment projects. Our Vendor Lifecycle Management (VLM) process systematically manages the entire vendor relationship, from selection and onboarding to performance monitoring, compliance management, and offboarding. By using quantifiable criteria, we ensure unbiased business award decisions and effective vendor management to control costs, mitigate risks, and build sustainable partnerships.

jobs.description:
The Global Logistics Exports organization enables customers across the world to access international selection that is sold in other countries. To do so, we manage the global cross-border supply chain, operating the largest Amazon cross-country network spanning +1k lanes and 200 countries. . By incorporating various programs and businesses into our supply chains, we spin the flywheel to reduce costs and improve delivery speeds for all our clients.

We are looking for a Business Analyst to join our growing organization. In this role, you will lead the development and optimization of Business Intelligence metrics that drive decision-making across our operations. You will partner with Tech, Operations, and Business teams to define key performance indicators, develop data pipelines, create QuickSuite dashboards, and translate complex data into actionable insights that inform product strategy and operational excellence.

The ideal candidate thrives in ambiguous environments, has strong analytical and technical skills, and can communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. You will be responsible for monitoring business performance, identifying risks and opportunities, and delivering diagnostic analytics that influence multiple teams' strategic decisions.

Key job responsibilities
- Partner with Product Managers, engineers, and operations teams to define, track, and analyze key performance metrics for cross-border supply chain products
- Conduct root cause analysis on business performance issues and provide data-driven recommendations to leadership
- Develop and maintain QuickSuite dashboards that provide visibility into product performance, operational efficiency, and customer experience
- Write complex SQL queries in Redshift to extract and analyze large datasets, identifying trends and opportunities for improvement
- Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable insights for technical teams, operations partners, and senior leadership
- Monitor business metrics proactively, flagging risks before they impact customers
- Collaborate with Business Intelligence Engineers and Data Engineers to improve data infrastructure

A day in the life
Your day starts by monitoring cross-border supply chain dashboards, identifying performance anomalies across our global network. You'll spend time writing SQL queries in Redshift to investigate root causes, then meet with Product Managers to discuss findings. Afternoons often involve building new QuickSuite visualizations or presenting diagnostic analytics to operations partners. You'll collaborate with Data Engineers to improve data pipelines and document your analytical methodologies for the team.

About the team
Join a growing analytics team supporting Amazon's largest cross-border logistics network. We work at the intersection of technology, operations, and strategy, partnering with teams across the globe. Our team values curiosity, data-driven decision-making, and clear communication. You'll have the opportunity to influence product strategy and operational excellence while working with diverse stakeholders from engineers to senior leadership.

jobs.description:
Cette position est un CDD de 10 mois sur notre site de CDG8, Beauchamp.

Notre réseau opérationnel, reconnu pour sa rapidité, sa précision et son service exceptionnel, livre des millions de colis et de sourires aux clients Amazon chaque jour. Pour sécuriser notre réseau et assurer la continuité des opérations, nous recherchons des talents expérimentés et avec un grand sens du détail qui souhaitent rejoindre notre équipe en tant que Loss Prevention Specialist. Dans ce rôle clé, vous assurerez la sécurité en tant que service, pour nos sites opérationnels tout au long de la chaîne logistique afin de protéger nos collaborateurs et de prévenir les pertes.

Key job responsibilities
• Soutenir la résilience de nos opérations commerciales en contribuant à la planification des urgences et des crises pour les sites sous votre domaine de responsabilité
• Contribuer à l'identification des vulnérabilités (par exemple, pertes, expérience des associés, amélioration des processus) et définir les actions d'atténuation appropriées tout en assurant le respect des normes juridiques et éthiques
• Servir de point de contact et coordonner les mesures de gestion des risques avec la direction locale et les parties externes (par exemple, les forces de l'ordre et les organisations syndicales) pour toutes les questions liées à la sécurité
• Assurer la conformité de vos sites par rapport aux normes de sécurité mondiales d'Amazon

A day in the life
En tant que Loss Prevention Specialist, vous rejoindrez une équipe de professionnels de la sécurité et de la prévention en offrant des services de sécurité à nos clients professionnels au sein d'une zone géographique dédiée.

Vous effectuez régulièrement des audits de sécurité physique, enquêtez sur les non conformités, les incidents liés à la sécurité et les pertes. Lors de vos enquêtes, vous collectez des informations en surveillant les systèmes et outils numériques, en analysant les rapports de données et en menant des entretiens. Vos conclusions aident à identifier les vulnérabilités de nos processus, pour lesquelles vous contribuez au développement de mesures de contingence. Vous jouez un rôle clé dans le maintien de l'intégrité physique de nos sites en auditant et en contrôlant l'état de santé de nos systèmes et, conjointement avec nos experts et fournisseurs externes, en assurant leur bon fonctionnement.

Vous interagissez quotidiennement avec nos fournisseurs de services de sécurité externes pour assurer la prestation de leurs services de haute qualité et d’une expérience client fluide.

Vous agissez également comme point de contact pour de multiples départements internes et externes (par exemple, services juridiques, ressources humaines, fournisseurs de services de sécurité, forces de l'ordre locales) et en tant qu'expert en matière de sécurité et de prévention des pertes. Enfin, vous dispensez des programmes de sensibilisation et d'éducation à la prévention des pertes à d'autres équipes et collaborez avec diverses parties prenantes sur les politiques et les procédures à tous les niveaux de la direction.

About the team
Faisant partie de nos équipes Global Security Operations au niveau mondial, la sécurité et la prévention des pertes soutiennent la réalisation des objectifs commerciaux d'Amazon et le renforcement de notre marque en protégeant efficacement contre les menaces et en gérant les risques de sécurité et de prévention des pertes qui pourraient :

• Mettre nos associés et nos clients en danger
• Perturber la continuité de nos activités commerciales
• Causer des dommages à nos actifs
• Avoir un impact négatif sur la réputation de notre marque

Travaillant en partenariat avec nos parties prenantes, nous sécurisons la chaîne d'approvisionnement d'Amazon de bout en bout et promouvons la sécurité lors des discussions d'affaires. Nous le faisons en prévenant les risques et les vulnérabilités liés à la sécurité et à la prévention des pertes le plus tôt possible. Nous intervenons dans les incidents en cours pour en minimiser l'impact, ainsi qu'en enquêtant en profondeur sur les incidents liés à la sécurité afin d'identifier et d'éliminer les causes profondes, empêchant ainsi leur réapparition.
jobs.description_short: Cette position est un CDD de 10 mois sur notre site de CDG8, Beauchamp.Notre réseau opérationnel, reconnu pour sa rapidité, sa précision et son service exceptionnel, livre des millions de colis et de sourires
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Les qualifications préférées ne sont pas requises pour postuler à un poste chez Amazon. Si vous avez toutes les qualifications de base mentionnées ci-dessus, nous serions ravis d'engager une conversation avec vous
- Un diplôme, un équivalent de diplôme ou une certification professionnelle
- Expérience des systèmes de sécurité physique, des techniques d'enquête et/ou de la gestion des agents de sécurité sous contrat
- Expérience pertinente dans le commerce de détail, les entrepôts, les centres de distribution, les services de livraison ou la chaîne d'approvisionnement
- Expérience dans la collecte et l’analyse de données
- Permis de conduire

Amazon est un employeur engagé pour l'égalité des chances. Nous sommes convaincus qu'une main d'oeuvre diversifée est essentielle à notre réussite. Nous prenons nos décisions de recrutement en fonction de votre expérience et de vos compétences. Nous apprécions votre envie de découvrir, d'inventer, de simplifier et de construire. La protection de votre vie privée et la sécurité de vos données constituent depuis longtemps une priorité absolue pour Amazon. Veuillez consulter notre Politique de Confidentialité pour en savoir plus sur la façon dont nous collectons, utilisons et traitons les données personnelles de nos candidats.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

jobs.description:
Wir suchen eine:n Site Coordinator:in zur Unterstützung des Standortbetriebs in unserem Sort Center. Diese vielseitige Rolle umfasst die Koordination von Mitarbeiterevents, IT-Support, Projektunterstützung sowie die enge Zusammenarbeit mit dem Store & Waste Coordinator.

Key job responsibilities
Employee Engagement & Communication
• Koordination und Organisation von Mitarbeiterevents und standortweiten Ankündigungen
• Sicherstellung einer klaren und zeitgerechten Kommunikation an alle Mitarbeitenden
• Förderung einer positiven Standortkultur durch regelmäßige Engagement-Aktivitäten

Volunteering & Community Engagement
• Organisation und Koordination von Volunteering-Events für den Standort
• Zusammenarbeit mit internen und externen Partnern zur Planung und Durchführung von Freiwilligenprojekten
• Dokumentation und Nachbereitung der Volunteering-Aktivitäten

IT-Superuser-Funktion
• Erster Ansprechpartner für alle IT-Probleme am Standort
• Bekleidung der Rolle des IT-Superusers: Koordination mit dem IT-Support, Weiterleitung von Anfragen und Unterstützung der Mitarbeitenden bei technischen Fragen
• Sicherstellung der Funktionsfähigkeit standortrelevanter IT-Systeme und -geräte

Projektkoordination & Change Management
• Unterstützung des Change Managers bei der Koordination und Umsetzung von Veränderungsprojekten
• Eigenverantwortliche Übernahme und Steuerung kleinerer Projekte
• Nachverfolgung von Projektfortschritten, Meilensteinen und Maßnahmen

Proxy Store & Waste Coordinator
• Vertretung des Store & Waste Coordinators bei Abwesenheit
• Sicherstellung der Kontinuität operativer Aufgaben im Bereich Lager und Abfallkoordination
• Enge Zusammenarbeit und regelmäßiger Austausch mit dem Store & Waste Coordinator


A day in the life
Dein Tag beginnt mit einem Blick auf anstehende Mitarbeiterevents und standortweite Kommunikation – du stellst sicher, dass alle wichtigen Ankündigungen rechtzeitig bei den Teams ankommen. Als erste Anlaufstelle für IT-Probleme unterstützt du Kolleg:innen bei technischen Fragen und koordinierst komplexere Anfragen mit dem IT-Support. Parallel arbeitest du an laufenden Veränderungsprojekten, verfolgst Meilensteine und steuerst eigenständig kleinere Initiativen. Zwischendurch organisierst du Volunteering-Events, die das Engagement des Standorts in der Community stärken. Bei Abwesenheit des Store & Waste Coordinators springst du ein und sorgst dafür, dass operative Aufgaben im Lager- und Abfallbereich reibungslos weiterlaufen. Dein Tag ist geprägt von Vielseitigkeit – du bist Kommunikator:in, Problemlöser:in und Koordinator:in zugleich und trägst aktiv zu einer positiven Standortkultur bei.

About the team
Unser Amazon Transportation Services (ATS) Team ist essentiell, um Amazons schnelles Lieferversprechen einhalten zu können. Bei ATS wirst du Teil eines diversen Teams, welches eine zentrale Rolle für den Erfolg unserer Supply Chain spielt. In Zusammenarbeit mit Luft- und Seefrachtunternehmen, Straßenspeditionen und unseren Sortierzentren sorgst Du dafür, dass Amazon seine Kund:innen so effizient wie möglich beliefert.
Unsere Mitarbeitenden und ihre Sicherheit sind dabei unsere höchste Priorität. Und wir nutzen das Können unserer Mitarbeitenden, um Pakete schnell, reibungslos und nachhaltig an ihr Ziel zu bringen. Unsere datenbasierte Entscheidungsfindung und unsere Flexibilität uns den Bedürfnissen der Kunden anzupassen macht Amazon noch effizienter und innovativer.

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills.
